jokes.text: Time's up! My turn. - That was half an hour? - It's your timer.
jokes.setup: Monica claims their massage session lasted half an hour
jokes.punchline: Chandler reveals she was using her own timer, implying she cut it short
jokes.explanation: Clean setup/punchline with good economy. Shows Monica's competitive nature and sets up the massage storyline.

jokes.text: I don't like to brag about it, but I give the best massages.
jokes.setup: Monica claims she gives the best massages while literally bragging
jokes.punchline: The irony of saying 'I don't like to brag' while bragging
jokes.explanation: Perfect for Monica's character - competitive and self-aggrandizing. Simple but effective irony.

jokes.text: Aah! Aah! Aah. It's so good, isn't it? I don't know what I did to deserve it.
jokes.setup: Chandler is clearly in pain from Monica's massage
jokes.punchline: His pained screams contrasted with Monica's oblivious enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Great cringe comedy - audience can see what Monica can't. Sets up the central conflict well.

jokes.text: Say goodbye to sore muscles! Goodbye, muscles!
jokes.setup: Monica tells him to say goodbye to sore muscles
jokes.punchline: Chandler says goodbye to muscles entirely, implying she's destroying them
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay with perfect economy. The escalation from 'sore muscles' to just 'muscles' is brilliant and quotable.

jokes.text: She gives the worst massages ever. It was like she was torturing me for information.
jokes.setup: Chandler reveals Monica gives terrible massages
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to torture for information
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and vivid imagery. The torture metaphor is specific and funny.

jokes.text: I wanted to give it up. I just didn't know what it was!
jokes.setup: Continues the torture metaphor
jokes.punchline: The absurdity that he'd confess but didn't know what to confess to
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that builds on the torture metaphor with escalating absurdity. Great structure and timing.

jokes.text: For the first time, I'm in a real relationship. I won't screw that up by telling the truth.
jokes.setup: Chandler wants to preserve his relationship
jokes.punchline: The ironic idea that truth would ruin a 'real' relationship
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler - neurotic and self-aware. The paradox is perfectly character-driven and relatable.

jokes.text: Dude, look out! You almost crushed my hat. Sorry. And the bunny got away.
jokes.setup: Joey warns about crushing his hat
jokes.punchline: Casual mention that 'the bunny got away' as if this is normal
jokes.explanation: Absurdist humor that creates intrigue. The casual delivery of the bizarre bunny detail is effective misdirection.

jokes.text: This would be the place where you explain the hat.
jokes.setup: Joey's confusing behavior with hat and bunny
jokes.punchline: Ross directly addressing the audience's confusion
jokes.explanation: Meta humor that acknowledges the narrative confusion. Good economy and deadpan delivery.

jokes.text: And you'll make them disappear?
jokes.setup: Joey talks about wearing high fashion
jokes.punchline: Ross assumes Joey is doing magic tricks, referencing the hat
jokes.explanation: Decent callback to the hat mystery. The magic trick assumption is logical given the context.

jokes.text: Like you could find something this sophisticated.
jokes.setup: Ross doubts Joey's fashion sense
jokes.punchline: Joey's misplaced confidence in his magician hat being 'sophisticated'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey - delusionally confident about clearly bad choices. Character-driven humor.

jokes.text: Please, take those off!
jokes.setup: Joey has been wearing the ridiculous hat
jokes.punchline: Rachel's horrified reaction to seeing him in it
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat. Functional but not particularly clever or memorable.

jokes.text: Maybe she's with us right now.
jokes.setup: Phoebe discusses her grandmother's death spiritually
jokes.punchline: The idea that grandma's spirit is hanging out at Central Perk
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe spirituality humor. The mundane setting contrasts well with the mystical claim.

jokes.text: Right. She's on a new spiritual plane and she'll come to the coffeehouse.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's spiritual beliefs about her grandmother
jokes.punchline: Ross sarcastically points out the mundane nature of the coffeehouse location
jokes.explanation: Good Ross skepticism. The contrast between spiritual plane and coffeehouse is well-executed.

jokes.text: I just saw two people having sex in a car right outside!
jokes.setup: Somber discussion about death and spirituality
jokes.punchline: Gunther bursts in with completely inappropriate sexual observation
jokes.explanation: Great tonal shift and timing. The inappropriate interruption creates good cringe comedy.

jokes.text: Actually, it's kind of cool. Because it's like... you know, one life ends and another begins.
jokes.setup: Gunther tries to connect the sex he saw to Phoebe's grandmother's death
jokes.punchline: The absurd philosophical connection between death and public sex
jokes.explanation: Absurd but weirdly logical attempt to make the inappropriate comment relevant. Original thinking.

jokes.text: Not the way they're doing it.
jokes.setup: Gunther's philosophical connection between sex and new life
jokes.punchline: Ross implies their sexual technique won't result in pregnancy
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and perfect deadpan delivery. The implication is clear and funny without being explicit.

jokes.text: We were in the market... and she bent down to get some yogurt and never came back up again.
jokes.setup: Phoebe describes her grandmother's death
jokes.punchline: The understated way she describes the tragic moment
jokes.explanation: Dark humor handled with Phoebe's characteristic matter-of-fact delivery. Touching and funny simultaneously.

jokes.text: The last thing she said was: 'You get the eggs, I'll get the yogurt, and we'll meet at the checkout counter.' You know what? We will meet at the checkout counter.
jokes.setup: Phoebe recounts grandmother's last mundane words
jokes.punchline: Her touching but naive belief they'll reunite at a metaphorical checkout counter
jokes.explanation: Beautiful blend of touching sentiment and absurd literalism. Very Phoebe - finding meaning in mundane details.

jokes.text: Really? A purse?
jokes.setup: Rachel shows Joey a shoulder bag
jokes.punchline: Joey's shocked realization it looks like a woman's purse
jokes.explanation: Sets up the episode's main running gag but isn't particularly clever on its own. Functional setup.

jokes.text: It's not a purse. It's a shoulder bag. - It looks like a woman's purse. - No. Trust me, all the men are carrying them in the spring catalog.
jokes.setup: Rachel tries to convince Joey the bag is masculine
jokes.punchline: Joey's continued resistance despite Rachel's fashion authority
jokes.explanation: Establishes the central conflict well. Joey's masculinity concerns vs Rachel's fashion expertise.

jokes.text: See, look. Women carrying the bag.
jokes.setup: Rachel tries to show male models with the bag
jokes.punchline: Joey points out the female models also carrying it, undermining Rachel's argument
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy and logical observation that undermines Rachel's pitch. Economical and effective.

jokes.text: But it is odd how a woman's purse looks so good on me, a man.
jokes.setup: Joey examines himself with the bag
jokes.punchline: His inadvertent admission that it's a woman's purse while calling himself 'a man'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic - contradicting himself while being weirdly self-aware. The emphasis on 'a man' is great.

jokes.text: Exactly. Unisex! Maybe you need sex. I had sex a couple days ago.
jokes.setup: Rachel says 'unisex' to describe the bag
jokes.punchline: Joey mishears it and overshares about his recent sexual activity
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey misunderstanding with characteristic oversharing. The mishearing is believable and funny.

jokes.text: No, Joey. U-N-I sex.
jokes.setup: Joey's sexual misinterpretation of 'unisex'
jokes.punchline: Rachel spells it out, clarifying the misunderstanding
jokes.explanation: Necessary clarification that extends the joke. Good economy but predictable resolution.

jokes.text: I ain't going to say no to that!
jokes.setup: Rachel spells out 'unisex'
jokes.punchline: Joey still interprets it sexually despite the clarification
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey - doubling down on the misunderstanding. Character-consistent and extends the running gag well.

jokes.text: Um, no, thanks.
jokes.setup: Phoebe asks to come in to deliver bad news
jokes.punchline: Ursula's casual refusal to let her sister in during a crisis
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ursula - completely self-absorbed and inappropriate. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Didn't she die like five years ago?
jokes.setup: Phoebe announces grandmother died today
jokes.punchline: Ursula's confusion about when their grandmother died
jokes.explanation: Sets up the running gag about multiple deaths. Absurd but believable for Ursula's character.

jokes.text: I know I went to that already.
jokes.setup: Discussion about memorial service tomorrow
jokes.punchline: Ursula thinks she already attended a memorial that hasn't happened yet
jokes.explanation: Continues the absurd death timeline confusion. Good escalation of Ursula's obliviousness.

jokes.text: Then who's been dead? Lots of people!
jokes.setup: Confusion about who died when
jokes.punchline: Ursula's casual dismissal that lots of people have been dead
jokes.explanation: Great escalation and perfect Ursula logic. The casual tone makes the absurd statement funnier.

jokes.text: Plus I'm going to a concert. I'd invite you, but, um, I only have two tickets left.
jokes.setup: Ursula explains why she won't attend the memorial
jokes.punchline: She says she'd invite Phoebe but only has two tickets, implying she's bringing someone else
jokes.explanation: Perfect passive-aggressive Ursula. The fake consideration followed by the excuse is character-perfect.

jokes.text: Fine. Okay, enjoy your concert. - Thanks. Enjoy your funeral!
jokes.setup: Phoebe sarcastically wishes Ursula well
jokes.punchline: Ursula cheerfully returns the sentiment about the funeral
jokes.explanation: Great parallel structure and perfect Ursula obliviousness to appropriate social responses.

jokes.text: You look just like your son, Mrs. Tribbiani.
jokes.setup: Someone mistakes Joey for a woman
jokes.punchline: The implication that Joey looks feminine with the bag
jokes.explanation: Visual gag that reinforces the purse storyline. Predictable but functional.

jokes.text: What? Are you referring to my man's bag?
jokes.setup: Joey is mistaken for a woman
jokes.punchline: His defensive insistence it's a 'man's bag' despite evidence to the contrary
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment. Joey's stubborn denial in the face of obvious evidence is character-perfect.

jokes.text: Your makeup.
jokes.setup: Joey lists practical compartments in the bag
jokes.punchline: Chandler adds 'makeup' to the list, emphasizing its feminine nature
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and perfect timing. Simple but effective escalation of the femininity theme.

jokes.text: Do you take Vasa or Mooster Card?
jokes.setup: Joey needs to pay for the bag
jokes.punchline: His mispronunciation of 'Visa' and 'MasterCard'
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey malapropism. Character-consistent and quotable, though not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: What's the part? Auntie Mame?
jokes.setup: Joey talks about getting an acting job
jokes.punchline: Chandler references a famous drag role, implying Joey looks like he's in drag
jokes.explanation: Good cultural reference that enhances the femininity joke. Economical and well-timed.

jokes.text: You-and-I sexy?
jokes.setup: Someone says Joey looks sexy with the bag
jokes.punchline: Joey's hopeful but inappropriate interpretation of the compliment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey - immediately making it about himself sexually. Great economy and character consistency.

jokes.text: You know what? My grandma had the exact same bag!
jokes.setup: Joey arrives at funeral with the feminine bag
jokes.punchline: Monica points out her grandmother had the same bag, emphasizing its feminine nature
jokes.explanation: Good callback that reinforces the bag's femininity in an unexpected context. Well-timed.

jokes.text: Pulling flowers out makes the bag look masculine.
jokes.setup: Joey pulls flowers from the bag
jokes.punchline: His delusional belief that pulling flowers out somehow makes it more masculine
jokes.explanation: Great Joey logic - completely backwards reasoning delivered with complete confidence.

jokes.text: You know what? Strike that. My name, uh, actually, is Joe.
jokes.setup: Frank realizes he gave his real name to his daughter
jokes.punchline: His panicked attempt to take back his name reveal
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Frank's panic. The attempt to cover up is believably awkward.

jokes.text: It's like it's coming right at me.
jokes.setup: Everyone is wearing 3D glasses at the funeral
jokes.punchline: Joey's amazed reaction to the 3D effect during a funeral
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd visual gag. The juxtaposition of 3D glasses at a funeral is original and memorable.

jokes.text: He said, um, 'Nice to meet you, Glenda.'
jokes.setup: Phoebe chased her father from the funeral
jokes.punchline: She reveals she gave him a fake name, explaining his confusion
jokes.explanation: Good callback that explains the earlier awkwardness. Shows Phoebe's protective instincts.

jokes.text: Just because you don't understand something, doesn't make it wrong.
jokes.setup: Friends mock Joey's bag choice
jokes.punchline: Joey's unexpectedly philosophical defense of his feminine accessory
jokes.explanation: Surprisingly deep Joey moment that's both funny and touching. The earnestness makes it quotable.

jokes.text: You guys have to get used to the fact that Joey... comes with a bag!
jokes.setup: Joey's philosophical defense of the bag
jokes.punchline: His dramatic declaration of commitment to the accessory
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and perfect Joey commitment to absurdity. Highly quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: I'd walk in there, I'd be like, 'Yo, Dad! You and me, outside. Right now!'
jokes.setup: Ross talks about confronting an abandoning father
jokes.punchline: His surprisingly aggressive fantasy confrontation
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Ross getting unexpectedly tough. The contrast with his usual personality works well.

jokes.text: I kind of scared myself.
jokes.setup: Ross's aggressive fantasy
jokes.punchline: His immediate recognition that he's not actually tough
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of the previous moment. Great self-awareness and character consistency.

jokes.text: Well, at least you scared someone.
jokes.setup: Ross admits he scared himself
jokes.punchline: Chandler's dry observation about Ross's limited intimidation abilities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler deadpan with excellent economy. The understated delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: You'd think I'd want to rip his tiny little head off!
jokes.setup: Phoebe discusses her feelings about her father
jokes.punchline: Her unexpectedly violent imagery delivered in Phoebe's sweet manner
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between Phoebe's sweet delivery and violent content. The specific imagery is memorable.

jokes.text: As a masseuse and a human... I'm begging you, never do that to anyone.
jokes.setup: Monica gives another terrible massage
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's professional and personal plea to stop the torture
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with perfect structure. Phoebe's dual authority (professional and human) is brilliant.

jokes.text: He does not like it. He's in pain! - No, he's not. - Yes, he is! - What? - I'm sorry but, ow!
jokes.setup: Argument about whether Chandler likes Monica's massages
jokes.punchline: Chandler forced to admit the truth mid-massage
jokes.explanation: Great physical comedy timing. The forced confession under torture is well-executed.

jokes.text: And by 'we'... I mean society.
jokes.setup: Ross makes a comment about lying to each other
jokes.punchline: His awkward clarification that he's not part of Monica and Chandler's relationship
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment with nice economy. Ross's social awkwardness shines through.

jokes.text: Could you try it without the purse?
jokes.setup: Joey auditions with his bag
jokes.punchline: The casting director's direct request to remove the 'purse'
jokes.explanation: Functional callback that shows the consequences of Joey's choice. Direct and economical.

jokes.text: Do you sell these? No, these babies sell themselves.
jokes.setup: Question about selling the bags
jokes.punchline: Joey's confident sales pitch despite being at an audition where it's clearly a problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey obliviousness - turning a rejection into a sales opportunity. Character-perfect.

jokes.text: But I didn't read anything. I think we've seen enough.
jokes.setup: Joey thinks he did great at the audition
jokes.punchline: The casting director cuts him off before he even reads
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing how badly the audition went. Joey's obliviousness contrasts well with reality.

jokes.text: My boyfriend said he didn't like my massages.
jokes.setup: Monica cries about work problems
jokes.punchline: Reveals she's actually crying about Chandler's honest feedback
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Monica's fragile ego. The misdirection works well.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! You don't know me at all!
jokes.setup: Ross suggests Monica doesn't need to be best at everything
jokes.punchline: Monica's outraged reaction revealing her competitive nature
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica - her competitive nature is her core trait. The outrage is perfectly calibrated and quotable.

jokes.text: You give the best bad massages.
jokes.setup: Ross tries to comfort Monica about her terrible massages
jokes.punchline: His attempt to spin 'worst' into 'best bad'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that shows Ross trying to be supportive. The logic is absurd but kind-hearted.

jokes.text: So they could call the award, 'The Monica'?
jokes.setup: Ross's 'best bad massage' consolation
jokes.punchline: Monica getting excited about having an award named after her, even for being bad
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Monica moment - her ego is so strong she'd rather be the best at being bad than not the best at all.

jokes.text: I suck!
jokes.setup: Monica accepting she gives bad massages
jokes.punchline: Her enthusiastic acceptance once she can be 'the best' at sucking
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the character arc. Monica's enthusiasm for being the best at being the worst is character-perfect.

jokes.text: Oh, it's, ew, used.
jokes.setup: Frank receives lipstick as inheritance
jokes.punchline: His disgusted realization it's been used
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor that adds to the awkwardness of the fake inheritance meeting.

jokes.text: I guess then I would have to say C. Total abandonment.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's multiple choice question about how his marriage ended
jokes.punchline: Frank's matter-of-fact admission of total abandonment
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan moment. The multiple choice format makes the confession absurdly clinical.

jokes.text: I don't want the lipstick that much.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's increasingly hostile questionnaire
jokes.punchline: Frank's realization the 'inheritance' isn't worth this interrogation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Frank's breaking point. The economic delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: If she isn't, cremating her was a big mistake.
jokes.setup: Frank asks if Lily is really dead
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's matter-of-fact observation about cremation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor with perfect economy. The understated delivery of such a morbid observation is comedy gold.

jokes.text: Yep, lipstick and a daughter. Big day for you.
jokes.setup: Frank discovers he has a daughter after coming for lipstick
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's sarcastic summary of his 'inheritance'
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback and escalation. The sarcastic delivery perfectly captures Phoebe's hurt and anger.

jokes.text: Well, you know, in my defense, I was a lousy father.
jokes.setup: Frank tries to defend leaving his family
jokes.punchline: His 'defense' is actually an admission of being terrible
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reversal of expectations. Using incompetence as a defense for abandonment is absurd and memorable.

jokes.text: I made up a song to sing to you, but that made you cry even more.
jokes.setup: Frank lists his failures as a father
jokes.punchline: Even his attempt to comfort baby Phoebe backfired
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of his incompetence. The idea that even lullabies backfired is sweetly funny.

jokes.text: Sleepy girl, sleepy girl, Why won't you go to sleep? Sleepy girl, sleepy girl, You're keeping me up
jokes.setup: Frank sings the lullaby he made up for baby Phoebe
jokes.punchline: The song is completely focused on his own frustration rather than comforting the baby
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the setup. The selfish lullaby perfectly captures why he was a terrible father but in a touching way.

jokes.text: To tell you the truth... they had a problem with the bag!
jokes.setup: Joey explains why he didn't get the acting job
jokes.punchline: His shocked realization that the bag was actually the problem
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the running gag. Joey's surprise shows his genuine belief in the bag's awesomeness.

jokes.text: I just don't know if the world is ready for you and your bag.
jokes.setup: Rachel tries to let Joey down easy about the bag
jokes.punchline: Treating Joey and his bag like a revolutionary concept the world isn't prepared for
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that treats the situation with mock gravity. The phrasing is memorable and quotable.

jokes.text: Yeah, um, they're called wallets.
jokes.setup: Rachel suggests other, less controversial bags
jokes.punchline: Chandler's deadpan observation that those are just wallets
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler closer with excellent economy. The simple observation cuts through all the bag euphemisms perfectly.
